haiku:
six bridges broken
a rail line ends in silence
the strait holds its hush
headline: Iran and US widen attacks as renewed conflict shows no sign of de-escalating

haiku:
kyiv before dawn
a warehouse burns in the dark
sweepers clear the glass
headline: Kyiv hit again as Ukraine reports two dead in Russian strike

haiku:
at midnight, the fence
comes down link by rusted link
neighbors walk straight through
headline: Gibraltar ushers in a new era as border fence with Spain is removed

haiku:
gray hulls on the line
the strait narrows to a word
freighters turn away
headline: US naval blockade of Iranian ports goes into effect

haiku:
cicadas go still
smoke drifts through the olive groves
planes dip to the sea
headline: Wildfires rage in Greece, Spain and France during Europe's record heat wave

haiku:
the senate desk draped
in black cloth and white roses
one voice gone quiet
headline: Lindsey Graham, longtime South Carolina senator, dies at 71

haiku:
a sea of mourners
a son speaks over the grave
vows heavy as stone
headline: Iran's Supreme Leader pledges revenge for slain father and predecessor

haiku:
the truce paper torn
envoys shuttle through the night
carrying small hopes
headline: Mediators work to de-escalate US-Iran tensions and revive talks

haiku:
sirens in the gulf
desert bases scan the dark
drones cross the night sky
headline: US, Iran launch more attacks as mediators urge both sides to uphold MoU

haiku:
dawn over the strait
a harbor tower in smoke
tankers hold their breath
headline: US, Iran threaten more attacks as strikes continue, Trump attends NATO summit

haiku:
amid the ceasefire
keys to the city change hands
children out at dusk
headline: Hamas dissolves Gaza government, ceding civilian rule to technocratic committee

haiku:
through tehran at noon
a slow river of mourners
black cloth in the heat
headline: Mourners throng funeral procession for Iran's supreme leader

haiku:
photographs taped up
on the plaza wall — each name
read aloud at dusk
headline: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 3,342 as families search for the missing

haiku:
two hundred fifty
new citizens raise their hands
as the rockets bloom
headline: Nation celebrates 250th anniversary of American independence

haiku:
one day to the fourth
heat shimmers off the pavement
kids wade in fountains
headline: 'Extremely high' rates of heat-related ER visits as record heat grips US, CDC says

haiku:
in a doha room
tea cools between two nations
someone says progress
headline: US-Iran talks in Doha make 'positive progress,' Qatar says

haiku:
warm sea at midnight
even the deep currents hold
the fever of june
headline: Global oceans break June temperature record amid fears of 'uncharted territory'

haiku:
a child born at dawn
the marble court says again
this soil is her own
headline: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, ruling against Trump's order

haiku:
five days under stone
a survivor blinks at light
the whole street applauds
headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,700 as miraculous rescues offer hope

haiku:
the diggers fall still
two faint taps beneath the stone
a fist means alive
headline: Survivors of Venezuela earthquakes found in rubble as death toll climbs past 1,400

haiku:
the fragile quiet
breaks over dark gulf water
lamps stay lit onshore
headline: US strikes Iranian military sites after tanker attack; Iran retaliates

haiku:
islands in blue shirts
a whole archipelago
dances in the streets
headline: Cape Verde becomes smallest nation ever to reach World Cup knockout stage

haiku:
at anchor for months
sailors write letters homeward
the strait holds its breath
headline: UN pauses evacuation of 11,000 seafarers stranded in Strait of Hormuz

haiku:
the ground's long shudder
dust hangs over Caracas
neighbors dig by hand
headline: Twin earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 strike northwestern Venezuela

haiku:
cicadas at dusk
the vineyard rows shimmering
even shade is warm
headline: France records hottest day ever at 44.3C amid European heatwave

haiku:
outside number ten
a podium in soft rain
the door shuts gently
headline: Keir Starmer announces resignation as UK prime minister

haiku:
at the long table
old enemies pour water
from the same pitcher
headline: US and Iran open ceasefire talks in Switzerland as Hormuz stays closed

haiku:
smoke over the hills
names are read in the courtyard
olive trees still stand
headline: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 32 in southern Lebanon

haiku:
after the last shell
quiet settles on the hills
a door opens, slow
headline: US announces Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire after deadly strikes on Lebanon

haiku:
at first light, tankers
file through the narrow water
gulls escort them home
headline: Iran and US sign accord; Strait of Hormuz to reopen as blockades end
